title: Runtime Configuration, Secrets, Environment Precedence, and Sensitive Data · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective configuration and secret-flow contract; isolate the first causal fault in runtime configuration, secrets, environment precedence, and sensitive data and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in configuration and secret-flow contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.
Resolve CLI, Compose, env_file, image, shell, quoting, unset, empty, required, default, and literal-dollar environment behavior.
Grant only named services access to declared secrets and configs and verify file path, permissions, rotation, and local Compose limitations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, Compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration.

Inspect the Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for separate build arguments, build secrets, image environment, runtime environment, compose interpolation, configs, and application configuration. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary, keep Docker Build Runtime Config Boundary connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Credential Helper Rotation
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-credential-helper-rotation-ds9guz". Tailor its configuration-boundary.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Credential Helper Rotation.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Credential Helper Rotation into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Credential Helper Rotation, keep Docker Credential Helper Rotation connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-credential-helper-rotation-ds9guz
  - competency: docker-credential-helper-rotation
    artifact: configuration-boundary.yaml
    decision: credential helper rotation
    scenario-variant: 1
    environment: native amd64 engine with a cold content store
    fault-injection: send termination during an in-flight durable operation
    retained-proof: timestamped lifecycle transition and bounded cleanup result
    case-key: ds9guz
    change: |
      public-config: immutable-environment-map
      runtime-secret: mounted-file
      build-secret: buildkit-secret-mount
      logs: redact-values
      rotation: restart-with-versioned-reference
    verify: docker compose config --environment && docker image history --no-trunc candidate
    failure: Reject secrets in Dockerfile ENV, ARG, Compose interpolation output, image history, repository files, or diagnostic logs.
    repair: Separate public config from secrets, inject each at the narrowest lifetime, scan metadata, rotate the credential, and verify redaction.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Docker Credential Helper Rotation structural check is only one gate. configuration and secret-flow contract must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Credential Helper Rotation, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Credential Helper Rotation precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Credential Helper Rotation, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A debug build receives credentials through ARG and ENV; Compose interpolation prints them; image history and logs retain copies. Classify public config, build secrets, runtime secrets, redaction, and rotation. Competency probe: Use scoped tokens, credential stores or helpers, non-interactive CI login, logout, revocation, and rotation without command-line leakage.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Credential Helper Rotation, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: docker login credentials are always stored encrypted and cannot leak through process or CI output.
